# OneFile

> OneFile is a free, open-source web tool that solves file upload limits on AI platforms. ChatGPT Free allows only 3 file uploads per day, Plus offers 10 files max per message, and no platform supports direct folder uploads. OneFile combines unlimited files into a single formatted text file that can be uploaded to any AI as one file. No account required. MIT licensed.

OneFile extracts text content from uploaded files and formats them into a single structured .txt file with clear file path separators. The combined file works universally across ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok, Perplexity, and any LLM that accepts text input.

Key capabilities:

- Combine unlimited files into one AI-ready .txt file (bypasses per-day and per-message upload limits)
- Supports 50+ file types: PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, and code files (JS, TS, PY, Java, Go, Rust, C, C++, PHP, Ruby, and more)
- GitHub repository import: public repos without login, private repos via OAuth
- Text content import: paste raw text or notes directly
- Respects .gitignore rules when processing folders, auto-skips node_modules, .git, build artifacts
- Browser-based processing for text and code files (content never leaves the device)
- Server-side extraction for documents (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX): processed but never stored
- Completely free, no usage limits, no account required

Creator: Mohamed Wahib ABKARI, AI Engineer. LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/abkarimohamedwahib/

## Pages

- [Home](https://onefileapp.com): Main tool for uploading files or folders and combining them into one AI-ready file to copy or download
- [How It Works](https://onefileapp.com/how-it-works): Step-by-step explanation of the file combining process with examples
- [Supported Files](https://onefileapp.com/supported-files): Full list of 50+ supported file types across code, documents, data, and config formats
- [Use Cases](https://onefileapp.com/use-cases): Practical examples for developers sharing codebases, students combining study materials, researchers merging papers, and professionals processing reports
- [FAQ](https://onefileapp.com/faq): Detailed answers on ChatGPT file limits (Free: 3/day, Plus: 10 per message, Pro: unlimited), Claude limits (20 files/day), Gemini limits (10 files/prompt), and how to bypass them
- [About](https://onefileapp.com/about): Background on why OneFile was built and information about the creator

## Blog

- [AI File Upload Limits Compared: ChatGPT vs Claude vs Gemini vs Grok (2026)](https://onefileapp.com/blog/ai-file-upload-limits-compared): Side-by-side comparison of file upload limits, max file sizes, and supported types across all major AI platforms
- [How Many Files Can You Upload to ChatGPT? (2026 Limits)](https://onefileapp.com/blog/how-many-files-upload-chatgpt): Exact file count limits for ChatGPT Free (3/day), Plus (~80/3hrs), Pro (unlimited), with reset timing details
- [ChatGPT File Upload Limits 2026: Complete Guide for Every Plan](https://onefileapp.com/blog/chatgpt-file-upload-limits-2025): Complete breakdown for Free, Plus, Pro, Team, and Enterprise plans including file size limits per type
- [How to Bypass ChatGPT File Upload Limit: Upload Unlimited Files Free](https://onefileapp.com/blog/bypass-chatgpt-file-upload-limit-2025): Step-by-step guide to combining unlimited files and uploading as one, works on Free and Plus plans
- [How to Import GitHub to ChatGPT For Free](https://onefileapp.com/blog/chatgpt-github-import-free): Guide to importing any GitHub repository into ChatGPT without a Plus subscription
- [How to Generate Documentation from Code Using AI (2026)](https://onefileapp.com/blog/generate-code-documentation-ai-2025): Workflow guide for using AI to generate code documentation, including using OneFile to provide full codebase context

## Optional

- [Contact](https://onefileapp.com/contact): Support and feedback form
- [Privacy Policy](https://onefileapp.com/privacy): Data handling: text files processed in-browser, documents processed server-side and never stored
- [Terms of Service](https://onefileapp.com/terms): Usage terms for the free service
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/wahibonae/onefile): Open source code, MIT license, contributions welcome
